questions: Q1: Does the resolved tickets/index.ts preserve both ticket (library CLI dispatch) and staging (ticket command updates) intents?
Q2: Are there any technical changes required for the CLI repo beyond build verification?

answers.answer: Yes. The resolved tickets/index.ts (150 lines) has all 10 subcommand handler imports (list, latest, get, create, rerun, continue, artifacts, artifact, bundle, update-description) and the standard switch-based dispatcher. Both ticket's library integration and staging's ticket command updates coexist.
evidence: Read tickets/index.ts lines 1-40: all 10 imports at lines 1-13, ticketsUsage function, switch dispatcher

answers.answer: No. All 6 library CLI command files exist in src/library/. Resolution utility exists. Main entry point has library case. SKILL.md has Library section. Only npm run build verification needed.
evidence: scout/reference-map.json: all 6 library files + resolve-library-item.ts present
scout/scout-summary.md: main entry src/index.ts has library case at line ~98-100

statement: The CLI repo's single conflict (tickets/index.ts) is pre-resolved with zero markers. Both ticket and staging intents are preserved. No code changes required — build verification only.
